text: U.S. research on the effects of radiation from nuclear bombs on human health was accomplished through egregious violations of the human rights of Marshall Islanders. Further, it is very likely that fallout from U.S. nuclear bomb tests was carried by the wind onto other islands of Micronesia. It is urgently necessary that the classified documents concerning the testing, patterns of fallout, and effects on the health of people of the Marshall Islands and Micronesia be disclosed. Health professionals must also work to strengthen the effort of nuclear-armed countries to honor their obligations of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons in their commitment to negotiate nuclear disarmament. As a matter of justice, for the basic human right of health, the U.S. should extend Medicaid to citizens of Compact of Free Association countries in Micronesia.
